entry.title: Re: Disable duplicate function in FSM planning board
entry.content:
i dont think this is would work because i have the problem in the other way:

the property "allowOverlappingAssignments" is set to TRUE but the user is not able to duplicate activies.

so i think, there are further settings missing

entry.title: Re: FI: Openning balance upload
entry.content: In order to bring 1y or more of historical data into the new system with preserved links you need SDT-Selective Data Transition.

entry.title: Re: Disable duplicate function in FSM planning board
entry.content: i dont think this is would work because i have the problem in the other way: the property "allowOverlappingAssignments" is set to TRUE but the user is not able to duplicate.

entry.title: Re: CAP-consuming on-premise OData API : read ECONNRESET...
entry.content: Without the logs, any thoughts would just be speculation. If HTTPS with certificate validation is needed, then it needs to be setup in the SCC, e.g. by importing the correct System Certificate and trusted root CAs.

entry.title: Re: How to print Rebate Credit Memo with settlement docum...
entry.content:
Hi @DRS ,
I am also looking for an answer for the same query? Did you able to find ana answer?

@Lakshmipathi  : Anything you can help here?

Regards, RP

entry.title: SAC Story fails to load on second open – “Communication with the remote system failed”
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m facing an issue in SAP Analytics Cloud.
The story loads normally the first time, but when I close it and reopen it in the same SAC session, I get this error:

“Communication with the remote system failed.”

The story uses a Live Connection. After logging out and in again, the first load works fine, but the second always fails.

Has anyone seen this behavior or knows what might cause it?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: DMS Documents Status Mass Change
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

I'm implementing DMS in Public Cloud. In the Migration Cockpit, the templates for the Documents don't have any field defining the status...

The customer sent us back the template for the mass upload requesting that 35.000 documents shoud be "Releaed" and 9000 Shoud be "Inicial".  As far as I know, there is no APP able to Mass Update the status for documents, once uploaded...

Do you know how can we mass Upload/Change Document status in this situation?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: How do you integrate SAP Commerce cloud with S4 HANA...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question.

Here's the reply from the experts:

1. What needs to be configured for Destination Target in BO: Configuring Backoffice for SAP S/4HANA Order Management Consumed Destination | SAP Help Portal
2. Is there any Test API's that are available for integration testing purpose: External customers will not have access to test S4HANA system.
Customers can access APIs via SAP business Accelerator Hub: https://api.sap.com/api/API_SALES_ORDER_SIMULATION_SRV/overview
[These links are provided on the hep portal as well: Availability Check | SAP Help Portal]
3. Steps for configuring Client certificates in Commerce and HANA: Using X.509 Client Authentication Certificate for SAP S/4HANA | SAP Help Portal

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Replacing SMTP with Microsoft Graph API for Workflow...
entry.content:
Hello,

Sorry, is this the information you're looking for?

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/btpcfsec/3361873367.html?q=Configure+Microsoft+Graph+API

Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: How We Built a Scalable Mass Hiring Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM Using SAP BTP
entry.content: Is this a bolt on partner solution or something that is now offered through a customer's existing SuccessFactors licensing agreement?

entry.title: ICMR: Reconciliation balances do not match
entry.content:
Hi there,
has anybody any idea why there could be a mismatch between the view in the fiori "#reconciliation balances" and the detailed analysis. For the latter, I used the fiori manage machting documents.

In detail:
I want to check for  a specific consolidiation unit the reconciliation status and go to the fiori reconciliation balances, I filter on 012.2025 and my reconciliation case. It shows me the general status for one leading unit. Next, I pick a specific trading partner and see there are, for example 5.000 € difference YTD up to now as well as the same amount up to last matching. (see screenshot). We are in € the same time, currency translation doesn't play a role. 
When I cklick on the arrow, I cannot find the difference, of 5.000€ in manage assignments. Here there is only one unassigned item of 300€, all else seemd to have auto-matched already. More importantly, when I do a detailed analysis of matching documents of these two trading partners,  I can trace the one unassigned item of e.g. 300€ that showed up in manage assignments, everything else seems fine (ok, I exclude reason code S98 - balance carryforward, but that's seems sensible to me). So only the 300€ remain, I cannot find these 5.000€ at all. Moreover, the totals do not match with the total in the overview. The totals are a little higher. And yes, I picked the right filters (recon. cases, period, year, trading partners etc.)

Any explanations for that? Why does the reconcilaition balance show me completely different numbers than the detailed analysis? My hypothesis is: It shows an older status and does not refresh, this would also explain why we have now a total for both trading partners that is a little more than the total shown in the reconciliaton balance status. Any experience with that?
@HengyeZhu @vincezk

entry.title: How to Validate Business Partner’s CEP (Brazilian postal code) through Extensibility using ABAP
entry.content:
How to Validate Business Partner’s CEP (Brazilian postal code) through Extensibility using ABAP

SAP Solutions:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition (SAP Cloud ERP).
  • ABAP Development Tools (ADT) for Eclipse.

Third party API:

              This blog utilizes the API https://viacep.com.br/. This is a free-to-use API provided by a third party, thus is not under any SAP responsibility. Keep in mind that its usage for CEP validation is at your own risk and that you can use any API of your choice.

Knowledge Required:

  • How to connect ADT for Eclipse to an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.
    • Connect to the ABAP System | SAP Help Portal
  • How to create an ABAP Class using ADT for Eclipse.
    • How to create a Class in ABAP 00 - 6 Steps to start | SAP Community
    • How to create a class in ABAP 00 - example code, with little typing. AdT is great! | SAP Community

Step I – Create an ABAP Class

              First of all, we need to create an ABAP class that makes the requests to the API. It should also handle the API response, which is in JSON format. Below, you will find a sample implementation of this class, but keep in mind that this is just an example and can be adjusted as needed.

CLASS zbl_cl_get_request DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_address_br,
             cep         TYPE c LENGTH 9,
             logradouro  TYPE c LENGTH 100,
             complemento TYPE c LENGTH 100,
             bairro      TYPE c LENGTH 60,
             localidade  TYPE c LENGTH 40,
             uf          TYPE c LENGTH 2,
             estado      TYPE c LENGTH 40,
             regiao      TYPE c LENGTH 20,
             ibge        TYPE c LENGTH 10,
             gia         TYPE c LENGTH 10,
             ddd         TYPE c LENGTH 3,
             siafi       TYPE c LENGTH 10,
             unidade     TYPE c LENGTH 50,
           END OF ty_address_br.

    TYPES: tt_address_br TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_address_br WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    CLASS-METHODS create
      RETURNING VALUE(ro_instance) TYPE REF TO zbl_cl_get_request.
    CLASS-METHODS get_data
      IMPORTING iv_postal_code TYPE string
      RETURNING VALUE(rs_data) TYPE ty_address_br .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS ZBL_CL_GET_REQUEST IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_data.
    DATA ls_viacep TYPE ty_address_br.
    TRY.
        DATA(lo_destination) = cl_http_destination_provider=>create_by_url(  'https://viacep.com.br'  ).
      CATCH cx_http_dest_provider_error INTO DATA(lo_http_dest_provider_error).
        "handle exception
        DATA(lv_http_dest_provider_error) = lo_http_dest_provider_error->get_text( ).
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        DATA(lo_http_client) = cl_web_http_client_manager=>create_by_http_destination( lo_destination ).
      CATCH cx_web_http_client_error INTO DATA(lo_web_http_client_error).
        "handle exception
        DATA(lv_web_http_client_error) = lo_web_http_client_error->get_text( ).
    ENDTRY.

    " Set HTTP Method (GET, POST, etc.)
    DATA(lo_request) = lo_http_client->get_http_request( ).
    DATA(lv_uri) = '/ws/' && iv_postal_code && '/json/'.
    lo_request->set_uri_path( lv_uri ).
    " Send Request
    TRY.
        DATA(lo_response) = lo_http_client->execute( i_method = if_web_http_client=>get ).
      CATCH cx_web_http_client_error INTO lo_web_http_client_error.
        "handle exception
        lv_web_http_client_error = lo_web_http_client_error->get_text( ).
        "handle exception
    ENDTRY.

    " Get Response
    DATA(lv_resp_satus) = lo_response->get_status( ).
    DATA(lv_resp_text) = lo_response->get_text( ).
    /ui2/cl_json=>deserialize(
      EXPORTING json = lv_resp_text
      CHANGING  data = ls_viacep
    ).
    rs_data = ls_viacep.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD create.
    CREATE OBJECT ro_instance.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

Step II – Enable this Class to be Used in Key-User Extensibility

              To use this class in the Custom Logic app inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the property “Use in Key User apps”, under “API State”, must be set as “Yes”. This must be done in ADT for Eclipse.

Step III – Implement the Custom Logic

  1. In the customizing tenant of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, go to the Custom Logic app (F6957) and click on the “Create” button.
  2. Choose the BAdI “Validate BP BADI” (ID: “CMD_VALIDATE_BP”).
  3. Give it a name and description.
  4. Open the implementation and click on the button “Open Code Editor”.
  5. Add the sample code below.
  6. Publish the code, then start testing.

Again, this is just an example and can be adjusted as needed.

DATA: ls_bp_address LIKE LINE OF it_bp_address,
           ls_message    LIKE LINE OF ct_validationmessage,
           lv_message    TYPE string,
          lv_postalcode TYPE string.
*Clearing the variants, just in case
CLEAR: lv_message, lv_postalcode.
*Fiiling ls_bp_address with it_bp_address data
LOOP AT it_bp_address INTO ls_bp_address.
*Attributing the value for lv_postalcode
  lv_postalcode = ls_bp_address-postalcode.
*Removing the '-' from the CEP
  REPLACE ALL OCCURRENCES OF SUBSTRING '-' IN lv_postalcode WITH ' '.
*Checking if the BP country is Brazil
  IF ( ls_bp_address-country = 'BR' OR ls_bp_address-country = 'Brazil' ).
    DATA(lo_srv) = zbl_cl_get_request=>create( ).
    DATA(ls_data) = lo_srv->get_data( iv_postal_code = lv_postalcode ).
    IF ls_data IS NOT INITIAL.
*Cheking if the region from the CEP's code list is differnent from the one informed in the BP Master Data
      IF NOT ls_data-uf = ls_bp_address-region.
        CLEAR ls_message.
        ls_message-msgty = 'E'.
        ls_message-msgv1 = |Region informed: { ls_bp_address-region }. Region CEP: { ls_data-uf }|.
        APPEND ls_message TO ct_validationmessage.
      ENDIF.
*Cheking if the city from the CEP's code list is differnt from the one informed in the BP Master Data
      IF NOT ls_data-localidade = ls_bp_address-cityname.
        CLEAR ls_message.
        ls_message-msgty = 'E'.
        ls_message-msgv1 = |City informed: { ls_bp_address-cityname }. City CEP: { ls_data-localidade }|.
        APPEND ls_message TO ct_validationmessage.
      ENDIF.
*Cheking if the street from the CEP's code list is differnt from the one informed in the BP Master Data
      IF NOT ls_data-logradouro = ls_bp_address-streetname.
        CLEAR ls_message.
        ls_message-msgty = 'E'.
        ls_message-msgv1 = |Street informed: { ls_bp_address-streetname }. Street CEP: { ls_data-logradouro }|.
        APPEND ls_message TO ct_validationmessage.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

Step IV – Transport

After testing, you can transport it.

  • Creating Extension Items and Transporting Them | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: KSeF implementation in CIM (DRC ↔ CIM integration for Poland)
entry.content:
Hi All,

This weekend, CIM introduced the option to connect DRC with CIM for Poland, enabling integration with KSeF (National e-Invoicing System).

As far as I can see, the only available documentation so far is here:
https://help.sap.com/docs/CENTRAL_INVOICE_MANAGEMENT/38e7dd38764346d995e098282320effc/7614cc7d448a48b89227dbfeb2b4b905.html?locale=en-US

I have a few questions:

  1. Are there any additional materials, guides, or implementation notes available (beyond the help.sap.com documentation)?

  2. Regarding “Preparing Master Data Files for Import” – do you know what the correct file format should be?

    • In the sample data and in the data structure documentation, the values differ.

    • For example, what should be maintained in the Exchange System field: KSeF or sKSeF?

If anyone has already gone through the setup or testing, I’d really appreciate your insights.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: BOM Status with Change Number
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

In BOM creation for Public Cloud, my understanding was: Status 1 is the generic for BOM we want to use in Production, while Status 3 would allow creation of BOM with change numbers... 

I am implementing Public Cloud in a company that keeps track of BOM's changes (with change number), so I tryed new creation with Status 3, but it doesn't allow to create Production orders stating the error message:

"No valid production version found for material 5820-40030000

Message no. C2142"

If I simply change BOM's Status to "1",  it allows order creation with no problem.

Could anyone help me understanding the main diference between Status "1 - Active" and Status "3 - Active with History" and the generic use of Status 3 in Public Cloud?

Thanks!

entry.title: How to delete Email Categories in SAP Emarsys
entry.content:
Hello, SAP Team,

We would like to understand whether it is possible to remove email categories that were previously created in Emarsys and, if so, what the correct procedure is to perform this removal.

Can the removal of categories be carried out directly through the Emarsys interface, or can it only be done by the SAP team?

We were unable to find any official documentation describing the process for removing email categories, only documentation explaining how to create them.

Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to delete Email Categories in SAP Emarsys
entry.content:
Hello,

I can confirm that currently there is no option to delete the link and email categories from the UI. However, this task can be performed from support side.

  • Reference: 3667965 - Delete link categories and email categories via UI - SAP for Me

Best regards,

SAP Technical Support

entry.title: How to freeze book inventory in WM during Physical Inventory (similar to MI01)?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
We are currently using the SAP WM module and planning to perform a physical inventory. In Inventory Management (IM), specifically in transaction MI01, there is an option to "Freeze Book Inventory" which allows us to take a snapshot of the stock while continuing warehouse movements.
Is there a similar feature or a specific indicator in WM (like in transaction LI01N or LI11N) to achieve the same result? I want to ensure that any movements occurring after the document creation don't affect the reference book quantity used for difference calculation.
Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: SAP Earns Silver Stevie® Award in “Best Predictive Customer Service Using AI”
entry.content:
We are excited to share that SAP has been honored with a Silver Stevie® Award in the category Best Predictive Customer Service Using AI. This recognition celebrates our work in applying AI‑powered predictive technologies to anticipate customer needs, prevent issues, and deliver proactive support.

 

What Are the Stevie® Awards?
The Stevie® Awards are among the world’s premier business awards, created in 2002 to recognize outstanding achievements of organizations and professionals around the globe. They have become one of the most coveted distinctions in business, with more than 12,000 nominations submitted each year.

They are often referred to as the “Oscars of the business world”, reflecting their reputation for honoring excellence and innovation across industries. [stevieawards.com] [businesschief.com]

 

Why SAP Was Recognized
Because of the high scores awarded by the judges, our nomination “Next‑Level Support: Leveraging AI to Predict and Prevent Customer Challenges” received the Silver Stevie® Award in the 20th annual Stevie® Awards for Sales & Customer Service. This category honors organizations that have most effectively used AI to anticipate customer needs and proactively deliver support.

Since July 2023, SAP has advanced its support model by introducing a preventive risk mitigation approach – a shift from resolving issues after they occur to predicting and preventing them before they impact customers. By analyzing system health, historical trends, and critical events, AI helps us identify risks early and alert customers ahead of time.

This approach delivers clear benefits:

  • Proactive prevention of disruptions during seasonal peaks and other business‑critical periods.
  • Improved user experience by identifying outdated components, such as browser versions, that could affect system access after updates.
  • Greater stability and trust through fewer customer‑initiated incidents and stronger business continuity.

 

Building on a Strong Track Record
This Silver award adds to recent recognitions, including:

  • Best Use of Predictive AI at the Enterprise AI for CX Summit 2025.
  • Bronze Stevie® Award for Best Use of Technology in Customer Service.

 

Looking Ahead
The award reinforces SAP’s leadership in redefining customer support with AI, automation, and predictive intelligence. We will continue expanding our AI‑driven insights and scaling preventive capabilities – helping customers operate with greater confidence, resilience, and efficiency.

 

Press play to learn how SAP Support brings innovation and reliability together - so you can move faster with confidence.

Thank you for the blog, Eduarda. Very useful content.

Best regards,

Marcos

entry.title: Re: PMW with 4110 UTF-8 code page for arabic symbols displayed as dots in S4 Cloud Public
entry.content: Unicode is the best choice for mixing any kind of characters (Arabic, English, Chinese, etc.), and UTF-8, UTF-16LE, UTF-16BE, etc. are the possible encodings for Unicode, you may choose any UTF format that is supported end to end. If there's an issue somewhere with UTF-8, you must identify at which exact step during the transfer there is a conversion from UTF-8 to something else.

entry.title: Re: mm02 attachments in smartforms
entry.content:
DATA: lt_attachments TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF sofolenti1,
ls_attachment TYPE sofolenti1,
lv_objtype TYPE swo_objtyp VALUE 'BUS1001', "Material
lv_objkey TYPE swo_typeid,
lv_bin_data TYPE solix_tab,
lv_xstring TYPE xstring,
lv_bmp TYPE xstring.

"Material number padded
lv_objkey = |{ lv_matnr ALPHA = IN }|.

"Get GOS attachments for material
CALL FUNCTION 'SO_OBJECT_GET_ALL'
EXPORTING
object_type = lv_objtype
object_key = lv_objkey
TABLES
object_content = lt_attachments
EXCEPTIONS
OTHERS = 1.

"Pick first JPG/PNG attachment
LOOP AT lt_attachments INTO ls_attachment
WHERE file_ext = 'JPG'
OR file_ext = 'JPEG'
OR file_ext = 'PNG'.
EXIT.
ENDLOOP.

IF sy-subrc = 0.

"Read attachment binary
CALL FUNCTION 'SO_DOCUMENT_READ_API1'
EXPORTING
document_id = ls_attachment-doc_id
TABLES
contents_hex = lv_bin_data.

"Convert SOLIX to XSTRING
CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BINARY_TO_XSTRING'
EXPORTING
input_length = ls_attachment-doc_size
IMPORTING
buffer = lv_xstring
TABLES
binary_tab = lv_bin_data.

"Convert JPG/PNG → BMP (SmartForms compatible)
CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_CONVERT_TO_BMP'
EXPORTING
input = lv_xstring
IMPORTING
output = lv_bmp
EXCEPTIONS
OTHERS = 1.

"Pass lv_bmp to SmartForm
CALL FUNCTION 'ZMM_XXX_SMARTFORM'
EXPORTING
iv_matnr = lv_matnr
iv_image = lv_bmp.

ENDIF.

entry.content:

We are unable to add new data fields to the email template FIN_CUSINV_EMAIL_TEMPL. We require two fields such as DocumentreferenceID and Customer name to include in the email. We are unable to find relevant business context to add new data fields to the template. 

Could you please provide a possible way to achieve to add required fields to the email template.

Thank you in advance!

And why this does not happen in the normal settlement just in the reversal?

entry.title: Unable to access practice systems in Learning Hub.
entry.content:
Unable to access practice systems in Learning Hub.

I keep getting the message under subscription '... EXPIRED', although the subscription has been renewed  , and the contract renewal end date has been changed to Feb 4th. 2027 under  the 'User and License Management' tab in SAP Learning.

Raised a case # 5000297284 on Feb 12th. 2026, but have not got a response so far.

Any guidance would be appreciated.

entry.title: QRC1 2026 Update Memory Problem - Loading Optimization Setting
entry.content:
Hi,

my Story uses about 2GB memory and has Background Loading active. After QRC1 2026 Update this story receives a Out of memory error at 5,4GB memory. After Loading the Viewpoint (Starting Page) it has 2GB memory used. 1sec after that the memory starts to increase rapidly and after 10sec the out of memory error appears.

I changed nothing in this story for months. It tried Viewpoint Loading and the same scenario appears. Only at Loading Optimization Setting = None no out of memory error appears, but the memory use is 3GB.

Override Cache Limit and Minimize Memory Usage is active.

PS: the same old Analytic Application used only 1,2GB memory. The memory usage of the new Story is extremely bad. I hope SAP will fix that soon.

thanks for help

entry.title: Re: OC SAP Community Group - Inaugural Meet-Up
entry.content: Thanks for hosting Vijayeta! This was a great opportunity to connect with others in the area.

entry.title: Re: Data migration in maintenance weekend
entry.content:
Dear @pauldorn,

Point 1.

Yes normally this is exactly what uptime is for Manage Prices Sales is the standard Fiori app to create/edit/copy/delete (incl. mass maintenance) sales pricing condition records

Important: Even during uptime, SAP can enforce temporary locks for certain activities around the upgrade most commonly extensibility/transport-related. If you can open the app and save condition records you’re fine but if you see system/provider lock behavior you may need to pause until the lock is lifted

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3564099

So, finish the condition load well before 22:00 UTC and leave time to validate pricing

Point 2.

No. During downtime the tenant is not available for productive work no reliable logon / processing

More details below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/understand-upgrading-and-patching-processes-of-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public/ba-p/13695428

And even more importantly around the upgrade window (including parts of the uptime phase) SAP explicitly restricts the two things you asked about:

  • Data migration

SAP states that during upgrade/maintenance periods the Migrate Your Data Migration Cockpit app is available in read-only mode and you can hit messages like maintenance event is in process so you should not plan migration loads in this window

SAP Note and SAP Community below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2996675

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/faq-on-upgrading-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13657921

  • Transports / Software Collections

SAP states:

  • Export during system upgrade: No
  • Import during system upgrade: No

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3126032

Also SAP notes you can encounter System is currently locked by the service provider for transport/extensibility activities even before the weekend upgrade window depending on SAP preparation steps

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3564099

Summary:

  • Use uptime for business activities like maintaining/loading sales pricing conditions in Manage Prices Sales, plus validation
  • Do not schedule Migration Cockpit loads or transport imports/exports during the maintenance weekend window assume they are blocked/read-only/unavailable until the upgrade is fully finished
  • Use downtime only for offline prep (files, checklists, reconciliations) not system actions

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Claude Code + Python + SAP GUI Scripting: Your AI Agent for Any SAP Transaction
entry.content:
I asked Claude Code:  "what is your opinion about the library? Can you create dynamically new scripts or steps to fulfill a lot of different actions in SAP GUI?"

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Good question. Let me give you an honest, technical assessment.

What the library does well

Navigation and field interaction — start_transaction, set_field, send_vkey, press_button cover probably 80% of what any SAP script needs to do. These are generic enough to work across any transaction.

Grid reading — grid_read_all with find_grid is the most valuable piece. ALV grids appear everywhere in SAP reporting, so this one function unlocks hundreds of transactions.

Screen exploration — explore_screen is a genuine capability multiplier. It lets you discover field IDs on unknown screens without manual inspection.

Helper patterns — run_transaction_report is a good abstraction. One function call can drive FBL1N, MB52, VA05, ME2M and many others with just different parameters.

Honest limitations

1. Dynamic script generation — partially yes, but with a catch

The library gives Claude (or any LLM) enough primitives to compose new scripts. If you describe a task in plain language, I can write a script using these building blocks. But there's a problem: field IDs are system-specific. The path wnd[0]/usr/ctxtFIELD_NAME can differ between SAP releases, customizations, and screen variants. I can write the logic, but I can't guarantee the IDs without seeing your specific system.

2. find_grid is brittle

It only tries 5 hardcoded paths. Many transactions use custom container names. If none of the 5 match, it returns None silently. A real dynamic approach would need recursive tree traversal.

3. No scroll/pagination support

grid_read_all reads only what's currently visible or loaded. For large result sets, SAP paginates the grid. Without scroll logic, you silently get partial data — this is a significant gap.

4. Table controls vs. GridView

table_read_cell exists but is basic. Many classic SAP transactions (MM03, XK03, FB03) use dynpro table controls, not ALV grids. These need different traversal logic.

5. No recording/discovery mode

SAP's own Script Recorder generates IDs automatically by watching your clicks. The library has explore_screen but no equivalent of "watch me do this once, then repeat it." That would be the real dynamic capability.

Can I create new scripts dynamically from this?

Yes, for a large class of problems:

  • Any report transaction with a selection screen + ALV grid output → I can write the script
  • Any transaction where you can describe the fields and steps → I can compose it from the primitives

No, reliably, for:

  • Complex multi-screen workflows (purchasing, goods receipt, FI posting) without first running explore_screen to discover actual IDs
  • Transactions with non-standard grid containers
  • Anything requiring pagination of large datasets

What would make it truly dynamic

The one addition that would change the game is a recursive screen walker that returns the full element tree of any screen. With that, Claude could:

  1. You navigate to a screen manually
  2. Call explore_screen_deep()
  3. Claude reads the tree, understands what's there, writes the targeted script

That would make the "describe what you want → get a working script" loop much more reliable. Right now explore_screen only goes 2 levels deep, which often misses elements inside nested containers.

Bottom line: The library is a solid, usable foundation. It can dynamically generate scripts for reporting/extraction tasks well. For complex transactional workflows, it needs either screen-specific knowledge upfront, or a deeper exploration capability to discover that knowledge at runtime.

entry.title: Re: Automating Probation Period Extension Using Intelligent Services Center in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi @VRNath ,

Thank you for taking the time to read the blog so carefully and for sharing these scenarios. I really appreciate the detailed points you’ve raised — especially around cancellations, overlapping dates, and back-to-back transactions.

You’re absolutely right that these edge cases are important when it comes to making the solution stronger and more practical. Your suggestions around refining the rule logic and handling these situations are very insightful, and I’ll definitely look into how they can be incorporated or enhanced.

Thanks again for your thoughtful feedback — conversations like this truly add value and help us all learn from each other.

Best regards,
Rishika

entry.title: Re: OC SAP Community Group - Inaugural Meet-Up
entry.content: Thank you @VijayetaSharma for getting this community started. I had a great time at the event!

entry.title: Re: Improving the SAP Community together, one comment at a time
entry.content:
Interesting discussion.

I agree the solution is not obvious, but if the scenario is "moderation team is working with limited resources and relying on their customers (?) to support internal moderating capacity", then the possible outcomes seem clear:

  • Good Outcome: Forum moderation keeps up with bad/inaccurate answers. The SAP moderation team deletes bad Gen-AI posts/answers and deals with repeat offenders, preventing them from contributing further. The community continues to be able to leverage Gen-AI content that is useful (as, when used responsibly, Gen-AI can certainly be an effective tool and work multiplier).

  • Bad Outcome: Forum moderation does not keep up with removing bad/inaccurate answers. LLMs begin scraping incorrect information from SAP forums, exponentially multiplying the problem over time and negatively impacting customer experience with SAP products. Forums become a minefield of right and wrong answers, increasingly less useful over time. To keep the forums useful, users are essentially forced to create a personal whitelist of trusted users for answers.

The variables that determine which of the above occur are "moderation capacity" vs "volume of bad answers"... and given the suggestions above of under-resourced moderation, I reluctantly agree with @Sandra_Rossi's belief that "Bad Outcome" seems more likely. In fact, from the linked answers @Sandra_Rossi shared, we're already seeing it, with people denying they used Gen-AI despite obvious unchecked errors and Gen-AI formatting, and no quick and effective action from the SAP moderation team in response (or worse, moderators accepting potentially incorrect answers on behalf of a question asker).

As Gen-AI will be scraping forum posts for future answers, it's critical to keep the "training data" (community posts) as clean and correct as possible. The greater the degree of error you have in the training data, the less reliable any Gen-AI model using data from here will be – including Joule, I'd assume?

Some suggestions for increasing effectiveness of limited moderation resources:

  1. Moderation Tool: Ban accounts and posts after violating the rules multiple times, but leave links to accounts/answers up, replacing them with a page saying "This post/account was removed for violating the following community rules: Validate Gen-AI answers for factual accuracy and/or disclose Gen-AI use." This will publicly communicate irresponsible/blind use of Gen-AI is not tolerated or valued here.

  2. Moderation Tool + Policy: For banned accounts/posts, set up an appeal process. While this may result in some false-positives (i.e. some useful posts are temporarily hidden, and get returned after manual review), it gives moderators a tool for hiding suspected bad content quickly until it can be properly vetted, and forces lazy/bad actors to put in more work and wait to restore their accounts and posts (disincentivizing the use of blindly reposting Gen-AI content or using bots).

  3. Moderation Policy: Clarify expectations and outcomes for the community. Announce a 3-strike policy for rule violations before an account is suspended from posting (or something similar), and require users to appeal to continue posting.

  4. Forum Feature: Before a user posts likely Gen-AI content, scan it and have a warning that pops up saying "Our system has detected the likely use of a Gen-AI tool in the creation of this post, but you have not added a Gen-AI tag. Do you certify that this was written only by you, without the use of a Gen-AI tool?"

As well, as this is generally a forum of professionals rather than the general public, hopefully we can consider that expectation of professional responsibility when making design choices in this space.

Dealing with irresponsible use of Gen-AI is an issue the entire internet is struggling with, so teams need to make the correct design choices before the issue becomes too difficult to solve as, without specific and intentional counter-measures, it will.

In the end, I greatly appreciate the hard work of the SAP moderation team, and really don't envy the challenge that this represents!

entry.title: Has there been an update to allow for curricula into a program?
entry.content: For organizations that have regulatory requirements but want to create learning pathways has the ability to put a curricula inside of the program been developed. This seems very much a necessity for organizations with heavy regulation requirement.

entry.title: Configurar processo de compra futura do zero
entry.content:
Boa tarde!

A empresa em qual trabalho deseja implantar o processo de compra futura, gerando duas NF's, sendo a primeira NF para realizar o processo da MIRO e a outra a  MIGO. 

Gostaria de saber se alguem poderia me ajudar nesta questão, sou novo neste mundo SAP e estou sozinho nesta demanda.

Grato a todos.

entry.title: SAP DM Production Process Design Read Indicator Dummy Value
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I have successfully integrated machine data from an ODBC database via Kepware middleware and visualized it within the Read Indicator of the Production Process Design.

However, I am currently facing a limitation as the middleware was operating on a free trial license, which has now expired. Management is in the process of evaluating the procurement of a full license, and as per the standard SOP, this approval process is expected to take approximately 2–3 weeks.

In the meantime, I would like to continue progressing with my learning and development activities. Therefore, I would like to explore whether it is possible to design a Production Process wherein dummy values can be assigned to the required tags in the read indicator. This would enable me to develop and validate an end-to-end Production Process Design in parallel, ensuring that project timelines are met despite the temporary licensing constraint.

Best regards,
Aziz Khan

SAP Digital Manufacturing SAP Digital Manufacturing Insights

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba Integration using SAP Cloud Integration(CPI)
entry.content: @rasjoshi Do you know how we can use the Full Load option? I tried the Load And Upload operation, but it still behaves like a normal Load and does not have full load functionality. It should display Load and Deactivate on the Ariba Data Import page, but it does not. @GunalanKG

entry.title: Re: Setting up Shop Floor Connectivity: Connecting your OPC UA Server with SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Very interesting article. I followed the same steps outlined; however, in our case, we were working with an ODBC database where data was being collected from multiple sensors at the work center level. Therefore, I configured an ODBC channel and implemented a query-based approach to retrieve and update the latest records accordingly.
Keep up the good work!

entry.title: Re: Preparing for SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 Release – Universal Full Profile View
entry.content: When registering for this event an error message appears that this event is full.  If this is correct, will this be recorded?

entry.title: ABAP SQL INSERT syntax Column &A of &B and the expression &C are not compatible for copy operations.
entry.content:
Hi, I've got this syntax error in my ABAP 7.58 SP 1 system:

          Column &A of &B and the expression &C are not compatible for copy operations. (message number INSERT 203 in table TRMSG)

at the line 7:

SPFLI-CONNID is of type NUMC 4 in the ABAP Dictionary.

What's the problem, how to fix it?

Thanks.

Sandra

NB: here's the code as text:

REPORT zdemo.

INSERT spfli ##LOGGING_VERSUS_FROM_SELECT[SPFLI]
       FROM (
       SELECT 'Z1'                       AS carrid,
              CASE WHEN connid <> '0400'
                   THEN connid
                   ELSE '0399'
              END                        AS connid,
              countryfr,
              cityfrom,
              airpfrom,
              countryto,
              cityto,
              airpto,
              fltime,
              deptime,
              arrtime,
              distance,
              distid,
              fltype,
              period
         FROM spfli
         WHERE carrid = 'LH' ).

entry.title: Re: ABAP SQL INSERT syntax Column &A of &B and the expression &C are not compatible for
entry.content:
The ABAP documentation of INSERT ... FROM ( SELECT ... ) says:

The columns assigned to each other must have the same type properties with respect to built-in data type, length, and number of decimal places, with the following exceptions: [...] All other types must be exactly the same. This applies specifically to NUMC and RAW, where the lengths must match. The different categories of strings cannot be combined either.

In the line 7, CONNID is fine (NUMC 4), but '0399' in the line 8 is technically speaking of type CHAR 4.

CHAR 4 has a "greater value range" (*) than NUMC 4, so the data type of the CASE expression is CHAR 4 which is not the type of the target column in the target table (SPFLI-CONNID of type NUMC 4).

(*) ABAP Documentation of sql_exp - sql_simple_case :

The result has the dictionary type of the specification with the greatest value range.

One workaround is to use a host expression @( ... ) to convert the constant '0399' in the right type NUMC 4 (NB: CONV can be used instead of EXACT if you prefer):

...
              CASE WHEN connid <> '0400'
                   THEN connid
                   ELSE @( EXACT spfli-connid( '0399' ) )
              END                                         AS connid,
...

entry.title: Re: mm02 attachments in smartforms
entry.content:
SO_OBJECT_GET_ALL and SAP_CONVERT_TO_BMP are clearly a GenAI hallucination.

Help - SAP Community: If you want to publish an answer that was helped/created by GenAI, you must include this statement: "GenAI was used to help generate this content."

entry.title: Re: Improving the SAP Community together, one comment at a time
entry.content: Follow-up: despite warning, the people who ignored my comments (answer still not fixed but GenAI label added by moderator hopefully) I mentioned above, persist in using GenAI without checking and not adding the GenAI label. I'll persist too 😉

entry.title: Re: How to enable Joule for the SAC Tenant Owner in S4 pu...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Can you please check whether the following information solves your issue?

  • Ensure that the SAC tenant owner has the necessary roles assigned. Even though the owner might not have roles by default, assigning roles like BI Admin or Admin might be necessary for accessing certain functionalities. For more information, see SAP Analytic Cloud Integration with S/4HANA Cloud - SAP Community

  • Double-check the user provisioning settings. It seems like you have already tried scheduling user and group provisioning. Ensure that the provisioning job is correctly configured to include all necessary users, including the tenant owner. For more information, see Avoid access issues during the initial set up of SAP Central Business Configuration - SAP Community.

Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: SAP Developer News, February 19th, 2026
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

SAP Developer Insights Survey

  • The 2026 SAP Developer Insights Survey is live now: https://sapinsights.eu.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_9prRAJk5ccYPMMe
  • 2025 Survey Report: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/the-2025-developer-insights-survey-the-report/ba-p/14323198

SAP RPT-1 Beta Available in the SAP Cloud SDK for AI

  • SAP RPT-1 (Beta): https://sap.github.io/ai-sdk/docs/js/rpt

SAP CodeJams in Australia and New Zealand

  • SAP CodeJam Group: https://groups.community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/gh-p/code-jam

SAP BTP, ABAP Environment Customer and Partner Roundtable

  • Event Details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/live-demos-at-the-sap-btp-abap-environment-customer-amp-partner-roundtable/ba-p/14328427

Did You Know #4: Released Object in ABAP Development Tools

  • More Details: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/7e27832a2d494e0b98359a7f1c6edefe.html?locale=en-US

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP Developer Insights Survey

0:44 SAP RPT-1 Beta available in the SAP Cloud SDK for AI

1:17 SAP CodeJams in Australia and New Zealand

3:15 SAP BTP, ABAP Environment Customer and Partner Roundtable

4:07 DYK 4 Released Object in ADT

Transcription

00;00;00;00 - 00;00;05;06
Announcer Guy
This is the SAP Developer News for February 19th, 2026.

00;00;05;09 - 00;00;09;22
Riley Rainey
Developers and SAP's annual Developer Insight survey has started live this

00;00;09;26 - 00;00;35;28
Riley Rainey
past Monday. You can get warmed up for it by reviewing Mamikee's blog posts in community. It's a complete report of the 2025 survey, contains all the data and also some key findings. There's tidbits on AI, popular frameworks help learning. It's all there. Have a look. This year we also have questions diving even deeper into those AI topics, we're keen to understand how you and your colleagues have been using AI for development in the past year.

00;00;36;01 - 00;00;44;26
Riley Rainey
We have links to the blog post and the survey in the section below. As always, your participation is key, so go check it all out. Thank you very much.

00;00;44;29 - 00;01;11;04
Kevin Riedelsheimer
The team behind the SAP cloud SDK for AI, released a new package to support the SAP Rapid-1 model. This package allows you to create a client to interface with the relational pre-trained transformer model. The package can simply be installed via npm, and is also currently only available for JavaScript. In case you haven't heard yet, but the Rapid-1 delivers accurate predictive insights from structured business data.

00;01;11;06 - 00;01;17;24
Kevin Riedelsheimer
This feature is currently in beta, but what are you waiting for? Try it out today.

00;01;17;27 - 00;01;19;02
Shilpa Shankar
We are live from

00;01;19;04 - 00;01;20;05
Rekha D R
Sydney.

00;01;20;07 - 00;01;21;04
Shilpa Shankar
Melbourne.

00;01;21;06 - 00;01;22;13
Rekha D R
Brisbane.

00;01;22;16 - 00;01;23;19
Shilpa Shankar
Auckland.

00;01;23;22 - 00;01;49;03
Shilpa Shankar
Hello SAP Community. We are in the second week of Australia New Zealand Roadshow and we just wrapped up ABAP Cloud CodeJam in this beautiful city of Auckland in New Zealand. We had lots of fun and lots of, technical discussions and ABAP Cloud topic because as many customers are moving to S/4 HANA and the feedback was tremendous.

00;01;49;05 - 00;01;57;07
Shilpa Shankar
Tomorrow we have another CodeJam related to Joule Studio in Auckland itself. Rekha can you highlight, what happened in Australia?

00;01;57;10 - 00;02;28;06
Rekha D R
Yeah, sure. So we started our week one in Australia, and we did, three CodeJams on Joule Studio topic. We started with Sydney and then we moved to Melbourne and then to Brisbane. There is a beautiful community, like all very technical people there. And we got to, try hands on with the customers and partners. And there is a very good response for Joule studio, product there.

00;02;28;09 - 00;02;54;28
Rekha D R
The community is very much excited for the, Joule and Joule Studio product and, yeah. And, in Brisbane we wrapped up, our, Australia trip with, SAP Stamtish (Meetup) with, wonderful SAP community, local community. So this way we had, lots of learning, lots of engagements with customers and partners. It was great, Shilpa.

00;02;55;01 - 00;03;14;09
Shilpa Shankar
Yeah, the local community meetup was good to meet, many local developers, in Brisbane. And, in case if you are interested to host, this CodeJam, you know what to do. Go to community.sap.com, go to SAP CodeJam group and then leave a discussion. We will get back to you. Thank you.

00;03;14;10 - 00;03;16;28
Rekha D R
Thank you. See Ya!

00;03;17;00 - 00;03;44;08
Mamikee Kanneh
Hi, everyone. Quick heads up. If you're working with SAP BTP, ABAP environment, the next Customer and partner roundtable is happening on March 3rd, 2026, from 10 a.m. to 11 a.m. CET. This roundtable is a part of a monthly series that takes place on the first Tuesday of every month, and it's a great opportunity to stay up to date with the latest products, insights, updates, and live demos directly from the SAP team.

00;03;44;10 - 00;04;09;11
Mamikee Kanneh
They also publish everything centrally on SAP community, including the agendas, the slides, the recordings and updates from the past and current sessions. So it's a great resource even if you can't attend live now. If you want the full details and the link to join. Check out the blog post in the link below to learn more.

00;04;09;13 - 00;04;37;28
Shilpa Shankar
Did you know that released objects that are available for developer extensibility are displayed in the Project Explorer of ABAP Development Tools under Released Objects in ADT. Open the context menu of an ABAP project and choose new ABAP Repository Tree. This allows you to create a new tree and filter for released objects. You can expand the tree by various options such as application component, package, API state and so on.

00;04;38;00 - 00;04;44;21
Shilpa Shankar
All the released development objects will be grouped accordingly. Under the properties tab you can check the release contract.

entry.title: From Conversational AI to Business AI: Why SAP Joule Changes Enterprise Process Execution
entry.content:
Generative AI has quickly moved from experimentation to enterprise boardroom discussions. But for SAP consultants, architects, and transformation leaders, the real question is not whether AI can generate content.
The real question is:
Can AI understand enterprise processes, respect governance, and execute transactions within complex ERP landscapes?
That is where SAP Joule changes the conversation.
Joule is SAP’s generative AI agent embedded across the SAP suite of applications including S/4HANA, Ariba, SuccessFactors, and SAP BTP. It is designed specifically for enterprise business processes rather than general-purpose interaction.
Unlike other generative AI tools, Joule is not simply a chatbot on top of ERP. It is an embedded enterprise process agent, designed to work seamlessly within SAP’s architecture, data model, and governance framework

1. Process-Aware, Not Just Prompt-Aware
Joule understands structured SAP business objects such as:
• Purchase Orders
• Invoices
• Journal Entries
• Sales Orders
It operates within live transactional contexts and respects user roles and authorizations. For architects, this means AI aligned with process design—not disconnected automation.

2. Native Enterprise Data Integration
Joule leverages SAP master and transactional data directly within the system landscape. It understands:
• Organizational hierarchies
• Role-based authorizations
• Process dependencies
This ensures accuracy, consistency, and alignment with enterprise architecture principles.

3. Governance by Design
For transformation leaders, trust is critical. Enterprise AI must meet compliance and security requirements.
Joule:
• Adheres to SAP’s data residency and privacy standards
• Operates within existing authorization frameworks
• Does not use customer business data to train foundation models for other tenants
Embedded governance ensures safe and compliant AI adoption.

4. AI That Acts — Not Just Advises
Joule moves AI from advisory to execution.
It can:
• Trigger workflows
• Draft journal entries
• Create purchase requisitions
• Guide users through process steps
This is where AI starts delivering tangible business outcomes rather than just insights.

5. Cross-Functional Process Orchestration
Enterprise transformation requires breaking functional silos.
Joule works across S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, Ariba, and SAP BTP:
• Retrieves HR data from SuccessFactors
• Processes financial transactions in S/4HANA
• Updates procurement records in Ariba
All within a single interaction. This end-to-end orchestration simplifies complex business processes.

6. No Training Required
Joule comes pre-trained on SAP business objects and processes:
• No need to teach it what a Purchase Order or General Ledger is
• Aligns with standard SAP process models
• Accelerates adoption without steep learning curves

7. Developer Enablement
Beyond end users, Joule supports developers by:
• Understanding ABAP and SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model)
• Generating code aligned with SAP Clean Core principles
• Supports innovation without compromising upgrade stability
This accelerates transformation initiatives while preserving system integrity.

Joule goes beyond traditional generative AI chatbots. Embedded within SAP applications, it serves as the user interface for Business AI, simplifying and orchestrating interactions and workflows across complex enterprise environments.

For consultants, architects and transformation leaders, the strategic takeaway is clear:
AI value in SAP landscapes will not come from conversations alone.
It will come from embedded, governed, and process-aware intelligence that drives measurable outcomes.
Joule represents a foundational step toward that future.

How are you approaching embedded AI adoption in your SAP landscape—and what challenges are you seeing in moving from pilot use cases to enterprise-wide execution?
We would love to hear your experiences, insights, and lessons learned.

Source:
SAP Help Portal | SAP Online Help
Discovery Center

entry.title: Re: Audit Logs Made Simple: Instant Access to the SAP Audit Log Viewer in SAP BTP
entry.content:
I think you need to update the auditlog viewer link in IPS, because now the 'default' URL has changed.

See your Cloud Identity Services tenant, append to URL: /admin/#/changeLogs/cloud

It automatically generates this link when adding the configuration:

Should be auditlog-viewer-initial-content.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/index.html

entry.title: list of vendors auto PO
entry.content: Does S4 have a report listing all vendor /materials setup for auto Purchase Order to  be created and emailed out?

entry.content:
Hi Akhila,

Thank you for your series, this helps a lot in term of understanding the concept behind BDC. To be honest this topic made SAP clients feel concern, and confused at some point. This series provide the reasons of the change in a very effective explanation.

Best regards,

Quan

entry.title: Re: Badi to maintain Attachment as mandatory at SES Level
entry.content: It will certainly remain so unless there is an agreement regulating the relationship between service providers and the source of attachments.

entry.title: What is the recommended learning roadmap for an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Administrator
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m currently working as an Administrator for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, and I’m looking for guidance on how to build a clear and structured learning roadmap for my professional development.

I have an active SAP Learning Hub subscription, so I would greatly appreciate recommendations on which Learning Journeys, courses, or certifications are most relevant for someone in my role.

My Objective

I want to understand which skills, topics, and SAP learning paths I should follow to grow from a foundational level to an advanced/expert level in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system administration.

Areas I want to focus on:

  • System administration and operations
  • Identity and access management (IAM)
  • Integrations (Communication Arrangements, APIs, SAP BTP services)
  • Extensibility (In-App and Side-by-Side)
  • Monitoring, lifecycle management, and support processes
  • Automation and operational tools
  • Best practices aligned with SAP Activate
  • Recommended certifications for administrators

 My Key Questions to the Community

  1. Which SAP Learning Journeys are essential for an S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition administrator?
  2. Is there a recommended end‑to‑end roadmap that shows how to progress from beginner to advanced?
  3. Which SAP certifications provide the most value for this career path?
  4. What combination of technical and functional skills should I prioritize to build a strong long‑term profile?
  5. Any practical advice or experiences from people who have followed a similar path?

I truly appreciate any insights, recommendations, or examples that can help me structure a solid career plan in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition ecosystem.

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: Journal entry - Add standard fields in Substitution rules
entry.content:
The documentation above referenced is specific to public cloud. For other products you can search in the help.sap.com documentation and review relevant product
Search Results | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Getting started with the SAP Logistics Management setup
entry.content:
@senol_kaya have you checked in your S/4backend Transactions /IWFND/ERROR_LOG  and/or  /IWFND/TRACES if you see any anything? Check for the user that is associated with the BTP destination whose name is indicated in the 3001 connection type (Manage System Connections app).

PS: you need to activate the trace first in your S4backend for said user, then trigger replication in LGM

entry.title: Re: End to End AI/ML Scenario Configuration in SAP DMC
entry.content: Interesting. Is this app still available in SAP DM (Manage AI/ML Scenarios)? If not, any alternative to this in current SAP DM offerings? Any related information would be much appreciated. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Assignment Profile - Assign only to new job classific...
entry.content: @louis_oli4, on top of what @jack_oneill has mentioned, you could also try using a custom field or the "Item Completion > Does Not Match" conditions in the assignment profile rules.

entry.title: Re: How AI in SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 Is Transforming CRM Adoption, Execution, and Business
entry.content: Great blog Luciano! This provides a great overview for any customers interested in the AI capabilities of V2.

entry.title: Re: Getting Started with ABAP Support in GitHub Copilot for Eclipse IDE
entry.content: Hi , I get below error. How to resolve this.

entry.title: Re: Creating a User Input Form in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Using a Planning Model
entry.content:
And for that reason just write else statement for error.... sorry, no love for this mess .... 

Just checking success is equally dumb as SAP not being CRUD at all 💀

 

Btw: making forms "like this" is rather bad as it is manual work entering entry by entry.

Making text boxes with copy paste and a little "text wrangling" for line breaks,  spaces etc e.g. and getplaintext and a similar logic per field or for all fields at once can speed up member creation and save worl time immensely. Also reading out what was entered and validity checks against members can be built easily. 

Maybe for another article as challenge 👀

entry.title: How Buyers Discover Suppliers on SAP Business Network
entry.content:
When buyers are sourcing new suppliers, they’re no longer starting with cold outreach or endless web searches. Increasingly, they rely on digital business networks to identify, evaluate, and short-list suppliers they can trust. On SAP Business Network, supplier discovery happens every day, often before a formal RFQ or RFP is even issued. 

But discovery on the network isn’t random. 

Buyers use a combination of search filters, category signals, profile details, and trust indicators to narrow down suppliers that meet their needs. Some suppliers consistently surface in these searches and attract inbound interest, while others, despite being registered, remain largely invisible. 

Understanding how buyers discover suppliers on SAP Business Network is the first step to improving your visibility, increasing buyer engagement, and positioning your business for new opportunities. In this post, we’ll break down how supplier discovery works, what buyers look for, and where small changes can make a meaningful difference in how often your business gets found. 

Methods Buyers Use for Suppliers Discovery 

Thousands of buyers use SAP Business Network to find the right supplier to fit their needs. Specifically, buyers can utilize two methods to do so: creating a public posting (RFX) and searching the network. 

Creating a posting 

Buyers can publish an RFX on the network with their sourcing specifications listed and get matched to relevant suppliers who meet the requirements. With a thriving RFX market, suppliers of all industries and sizes can generate quality leads for the business as they get matched to opportunities. Buyers posted over $X billion in business opportunities in 2025, so there is no shortage of sales to be made. 

Searching the network 

Alternatively, buyers can choose to search the network directly for the products, service, or specific company they are looking for either directly from their SAP Business Network account, or from their S4 public cloud application. They can then choose to filter their search results by business type, certifications, or verification status. Once they find a supplier they’re interested in, they can issue them an RFI. 

Click here to learn more about how this process works for buyers and the amount of business opportunities awaiting you on the network.

What Buyers Are Actually Doing When They Search 

Regardless of the method they choose, when buyers use SAP Business Network to find suppliers, they’re usually trying to solve a specific, time-sensitive problem. They may be sourcing a new category, looking to qualify alternative suppliers, or filling a gap in an existing supply base. In most cases, they’re not browsing, but rather filtering their search to find the right supplier. 

Buyers typically start with core criteria such as product or service category, geographic coverage, and certifications. From there, they quickly narrow the list by scanning supplier profiles for signs of relevance and credibility. Clear descriptions, accurate classifications, and complete business information help buyers determine whether a supplier is even worth a closer look. 

Just as important are trust and engagement signals. Buyers look for indicators that a supplier is active on the network, responsive to inquiries, and prepared to do business digitally. Profiles that appear incomplete, outdated, or hard to evaluate are often skipped over, regardless of how capable the supplier may be offline. 

The result? Supplier discovery on the network tends to favor businesses that are both easy to find and easy to assess. Visibility gets a supplier into the search results, but clarity and credibility determine whether a buyer clicks, engages, or moves on to the next option. 

What this means for you 

It's important to view your network presence from the eyes of your prospective customers. What is the information they are looking for to determine their interest in a supplier, and do you provide it in a quick and easy to understand way?  

Log in and see how you show up in buyer searches by heading to the search bar on your homepage, clicking on the drop down for the left-most field and changing it to “trading partners”, and choosing the search terms most relevant to your business. 

Check out the SAP Business Network, promote subscription to see how you can increase your visibility in buyer searches on the network.

Understanding how supplier discovery works is the first step. Optimizing how your business shows up is what turns visibility into real buyer interest.  

In a blog that follows, we’ll explore the specific factors that influence where suppliers appear in buyer searches and what you can do to improve your chances of being discovered.  

Subscribe to the SAP Business Network for Suppliers tag to stay updated on new posts and be sure to check out the newly updated homepage for suppliers on SAP Community.

entry.title: Using SKF's for reporting
entry.content:
Hello, We are currently inflight within our SAP S4 Hana integration project, and having the debate around SKF usage. We currently capture around 500 statistical data KPI's within our current system that are not used for allocations alone but also for reporting. These stats include tonnes produced, hauled, PA, UA, operating hours, etc. They are all manually loaded at month end into our primary costing system. 

My question, does anyone; that is on SAP S4 Hana, use SKF's to house their statistical data KPI's to enable not only allocations but also reporting? If so, do they function well? If you did, but then pivoted, what was the issue? 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Unable to access practice systems in Learning Hub.
entry.content:
SOLUTION in my case:

1. Go to the 'User and License Management' tab/screen under My Learning.

2. Click on the 'Assign Licenses' option.

3. The system will ask you to select an email id linked with your user id. 

4. Once the license is assigned to the email id the 'Assignment status' would change to 'Assigned'.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to calculate measures on property level and copy it to lowest dimension level
entry.content:
This +1 

No need for Data Action at all.

entry.title: Re: How to calculate measures on property level and copy it to lowest dimension level
entry.content:
And is inferior if you can have it on the fly instead

-1

entry.title: Re: Upload comments through CSV
entry.content: This is not what was asked. The requirement is uploaf by csv...

entry.title: Group Reporting 2023 On-Prem: Due to the latest upgrade, no ledger could be assigned to this version
entry.content:
Hello SAP Guru's

Upon accessing the Data Monitor or Consolidation Monitor in S/4HANA 2023 On-Premise, the system displays a hard error: "Due to the latest upgrade, no ledger could be assigned to this version. The master data is incorrect and therefore you can’t use this version."

This occurred immediately following the activation of SAP Best Practices content.

Thank you in advance..

Navendu

entry.title: Re: Upload comments through CSV
entry.content:
Make the Comment a dimensionless field in planning model. 

Now every free text can be accepted that gets uploaded. 

To avoid the field after 2, 3, 4 uploads to produce more than 1 row as you probably wish to overwrite you have to Apple that logic in the upload job setting. Since some time there in settings you can choose the replacement behavior and Keys. 

Or use old manual way - upload goes to some edit version (Actual*) 

Or private* Version. And you have a "purge" Button. Or replacement /deletion before reupload happens (data action ... ). 

Make youself familiar wirh "in built comments " , stuff changed there as well heavily that might help (also with AI comment aggregation functions)

entry.content:
How to load data from this extractor 0FC_OP_01 to datasphere?

entry.title: Re: Preparing for SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 Release – Universal Full Profile View
entry.content: This session is showing as full when registering, is there another session planned/available?

entry.title: Re: mm02 attachments in smartforms
entry.content:
Hi @Sandra_Rossi , thanks for your comment, Just to clarify for the OP:

  •  SO_OBJECT_GET_ALL is a standard GOS API for retrieving attachments.
  • SAP_CONVERT_TO_BMP is also a standard FM used when SmartForms require BMP/TIFF formats

These are older but still valid components in many ECC and S/4HANA on‑premise systems, especially in utilities and manufacturing landscapes where GOS attachments are common.

I shared the snippet because it’s one of the practical approaches I’ve used in real projects when customers store material images as MM02 attachments.

Happy to help if the OP needs additional context or alternatives.

entry.title: Re: Hi
entry.content:
Try this: instead of joining the full tables, join only on the document header + line item keys:

define view ZFI_ACDOCA_BSEG as

  select from acdoca as a

    inner join bseg as b

      on  a.rclnt = b.mandt

      and a.bukrs = b.bukrs

      and a.belnr = b.belnr

      and a.gjahr = b.gjahr

      and a.buzei = b.buzei

{

  a.bukrs,

  a.belnr,

  a.gjahr,

  a.buzei,

  a.hkont,

  b.shkzg,

  b.dmbtr

}

entry.title: How to extract data from IS-U extractor 0FC-_BP_Item into SAP Datasphere? Is there any way?
entry.content: I am extracting data from 0FC_BP_Items into datasphere through replication flow, but its is giving me 0 record? Is there any soultion to resolve this?

entry.title: Re: SAP S4 Hana Advanced Bank Statement rules - Document...
entry.content:
Did this ever get resolved. I have exactly the same issue on public cloud.  Even though the processing instruction 0010 is available on the dropdown selection to allocate open items on the reprocess bank statement screen,  it does not seem to work or even put any messages into the log.    If I create my own processing rule using clear in sequence and select 0010  I get the same result.

although in this case it does put a message in the background job log saying   "not configured"

I also have the same issue with 0080.

I am not sure if only interpretation algorithms starting with a 4 work, but at least they do run and  put a message in the log.

entry.title: Re: BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA returns Success but MARC-MAABC and MARC-ABCIN do not update (S/4HANA)
entry.content:
1.  The BAPI requires these structures to be present when updating plant data.

ls_clientdata-material_long = ls_out-matnr.

ls_clientdatax-material_long = 'X'.

2.  Setting only mrp_view = 'X' and storage_view = 'X' is not enough.

ls_headdata-basic_view = 'X'.

ls_headdata-purchase_view = 'X'.

3. If the material is not extended to that plant, the BAPI will not create MARC automatically unless you set:

ls_plantdatax-plant = 'X'.

4. Check the RETURN table for warnings.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4 Hana Advanced Bank Statement rules - Document...
entry.content: I dont think it is to do with the document ranges in the parameter set, as 0080 is on assignment field which does not have a range, and that does not work either

entry.title: Re: Confirming quantities with the EAN of the item at EWM...
entry.content: Thank you very much for the recommendations.

entry.title: Is encryption with HSM supported for ASE on IBM AIX?
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We want to implement encryption in our servers using a HSM key, but in version ASE 16.0 SP03 PL06 manual there is a note that mention that AIX and Solaris can not use this functionality.

We are running ASE 16.0 SP04 PL06. Do you know if this restriction continue in this version? 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is encryption with HSM supported for ASE on IBM AIX?
entry.content:
Starting from ASE 16.0 SP03 PL06, SAP ASE can use the Hardware Security Module (HSM) external keystore to encrypt keys and is supported on all SAP ASE platforms except IBM AIX.

At this time, encryption support for IBM AIX remains planned, but SAP has not yet defined an expected release date.

entry.title: Re: Google Pub/Sub Adapter in SAP Integration Suite:
entry.content: Nice Blog. Thank you for this insightful and well structured article on the Google Pub/Sub Adapter for SAP Integration Suite.

entry.title: How to verify ASE database size and allocation details in Netweaver?
entry.content:
• Is there a way to check database size/allocation without running OS-level commands?
  • How can database space usage be checked without using isql commands?
  • How can database space allocation be monitored through the SAP GUI?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to verify ASE database size and allocation detail...
entry.content:
Database size and allocation details can be obtained through the DBA Cockpit interface, where the information can be accessed without the need for OS‑level commands or isql:

Navigate to DBA Cockpit → Database → Space → Databases

entry.title: Re: RAP: Using state message causes error
entry.content: Were you able to solve this?

Henry
